channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Exploring Britain’s Most Unusual Landmarks and Curiosities
item.description: Britain is home to an extraordinary array of unusual landmarks and eccentric sites that reflect the nation’s creativity and whimsical character. From towering sculptures to miniature architectural rep...

item.title: Tanzania Launches Xi Jinping Thought Research Centre at Mwalimu Nyerere Leadership School to Boost Development Cooperation
item.description: On May 20, 2026, Tanzania officially opened a Research Centre for Xi Jinping Thought on Socialism with Chinese Characteristics for a New Era at the Mwalimu Nyerere Leadership School in Kibaha, Coast R...

item.title: Trump Surgeon General Nominee Criticized for Using Farmland Tax Loophole Despite Not Farming
item.description: Donald Trump's nominee for Surgeon General, Nicole Saphier, a Fox News contributor and radiologist, is under scrutiny for claiming a New Jersey farmland tax break despite not engaging in actual farmin...

item.title: Delhi Experiences Record High Night Temperatures in May, Authorities Issue Orange Heatwave Alert
item.description: Delhi recorded its highest night temperature for the month of May in nearly 14 years, reaching 31.9 degrees Celsius, according to the India Meteorological Department. Safdarjung and Lodhi Road weather...

item.title: Bank of Tanzania Increases Gold Holdings as Key Reserve
item.description: The Bank of Tanzania has stepped up its strategy of using gold as a key part of the country's reserves. As of April 30, 2026, total reserves reached 8.3 trillion Tanzanian shillings, with 5.1 trillion...

item.title: House prosecution panel conducts preparations for VP Sara Duterte's upcoming impeachment trial
item.description: The House Prosecution Panel is actively preparing for the anticipated Senate impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte, although no formal schedule has yet been communicated by the Senate Impea...

item.title: Naga Communities Protest Across Manipur Over Hostage Crisis
item.description: On May 21, 2026, thousands of women from Naga communities held multiple sit-in protests in various Naga-inhabited regions of Manipur, urging the immediate release of six men abducted by suspected Kuki...

item.title: Government to Launch New Likoni Ferry by December to Ease Traffic
item.description: President William Ruto has confirmed that a new ferry will be constructed at the Likoni channel, with completion expected by December 2026. The project is estimated to cost KSh 3 billion and is aimed ...

item.title: Neo-Nazi Organisation White Australia Seeks Legal Review of Hate Group Listing
item.description: The neo-Nazi group White Australia, formerly known as the National Socialist Network, has lodged a legal challenge against the Australian government's designation of it as a prohibited hate group. The...

item.title: Barrister Atauwo Job Urang Officially Becomes Notary Public in Rivers State
item.description: Barrister Atauwo Job Urang, the eldest daughter of former Director of Finance and Administration of Rivers State Newspaper Corporation, Mr. Job O. Urang, has officially been sworn in as a Notary Publi...

item.title: Bacolod radio anchor faces backlash for inappropriate on-air remarks about rape
item.description: A Bacolod-based radio anchor, Jean Paul Generoso of K5 News FM Bacolod 101.5, has drawn widespread criticism for making an offensive remark about rape during a live broadcast on May 20, 2026. Generoso...

item.title: Rio Ferdinand faces sportswashing accusations following visit to Tanzania amid human rights concerns
item.description: Former Manchester United and England defender Rio Ferdinand has come under criticism after visiting Tanzania and publicly supporting the country's government ahead of the 2027 Africa Cup of Nations. F...

item.title: TANAPA to Open International Golf Course in Serengeti to Boost Tourism and Local Employment
item.description: The Tanzania National Parks Authority (TANAPA) is set to inaugurate an international-standard golf course at Fort Ikoma, near Serengeti National Park, on May 28, 2026. This project is part of Tanzania...

item.title: India urges BRICS nations to strengthen global counter-terrorism cooperation
item.description: During the ongoing BRICS summit in India, Sibi George, Secretary (West) at the Ministry of External Affairs, addressed the 11th Annual BRICS Counter Terrorism Working Group meeting in Delhi on Thursda...

item.title: Column Criticizes Trump for Controversial $1.776 Billion Fund Linked to IRS Settlement
item.description: A recent Guardian column by Moira Donegan sharply criticizes President Donald Trump's handling of a $1.776 billion fund, calling it an 'extraordinary incident of bald self-dealing.' The fund, labeled ...

item.title: Government Plans Infrastructure Bonds to Fund Road and Transport Projects
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced plans to introduce infrastructure bonds as a new way of financing major road construction and transport development projects across the country. This was reveale...

item.title: Investigation Underway After Southern California Couple Found Dead; Possible Romance Scam Involved
item.description: Karen and Donald Whitaker, a couple in their late 70s, were found dead last week in their Bermuda Dunes home in Coachella Valley. Riverside County sheriff’s deputies discovered the couple after a neig...

item.title: U.S. Intelligence Reports Show Iran Recovering Faster Than Expected After Strikes
item.description: U.S. intelligence assessments indicate that Iran's military recovery is progressing faster than what U.S. and Israeli officials initially claimed. CENTCOM Commander Admiral Brad Cooper testified that ...

item.title: Limpopo Villages Remain Without Water Despite R486-Million Project
item.description: Communities in Chavani and neighbouring villages in the Vhembe district of Limpopo are still struggling without running water, even though a R486-million bulk water system was built to serve them. The...

item.title: Former World Leaders Simulate Health Emergencies to Improve Global Preparedness
item.description: A group of former world leaders, known as the Elders and founded by Nelson Mandela in 2007, participated in a health emergency simulation in Nairobi, Kenya, to explore how Africa is preparing for pote...
